text: Fill in the blanks about the posterior compartment of the leg and the sole of the foot.
questions: The calcaneal (Achilles) tendon is formed by the convergence of *gastrocnemius/gastrocnemius:two-headed calf muscle* and *soleus/soleus:deep to gastrocnemius* tendons. It is the thickest and strongest tendon in the body.
The avascular zone of the calcaneal tendon lies *2-6/2 to 6/two to six* cm above the calcaneal insertion — this is where most ruptures occur.
The Thompson test involves squeezing the *calf/calf muscles* — if the foot does not plantarflex, the calcaneal tendon is ruptured.

The deep posterior compartment muscles passing behind the medial malleolus are remembered by the mnemonic 'Tom, Dick, ANd Very Nervous Harry': *tibialis posterior/tibialis posterior:Tom*, flexor digitorum longus, posterior tibial artery, tibial nerve, and flexor hallucis longus.
*Plantar fasciitis/plantar fasciitis:commonest cause of heel pain* is the most common cause of heel pain in adults, especially in those who stand for prolonged periods.
